mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures aggregate consumer demand momentum; sustained increases in nominal retail sales drive corporate revenue expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when RSAFS increases sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, equity discount rates rise abruptly and corporate borrowing costs suppress earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a robust labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, amplifying positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either variable predicts alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) for 20-day returns above main effects alone.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) generates positive S&P 500 forward returns when monetary policy is accommodative, but the relationship breaks down during tightening cycles because rising discount rates offset output gains.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%, positive INDPRO momentum does not produce statistically significant positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below a critical threshold signal collapsing residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, durables, and credit, foreshadowing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1000 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a stagnant or falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) create negative real policy rates that inflate nominal corporate revenues and erode debt burdens, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equities reprice for financial repression.
failure_condition: Joint positive CPIAUCSL momentum and low FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 5-day returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor markets and rising wage costs that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors revise earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Periods of declining UNRATE do not correlate with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC magnitude < 0.03 over any 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings breadth; above-trend GDP prints produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions follow with a lag.
failure_condition: Positive surprises in GDPC1 do not generate statistically significant positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) during expansion phases.
